title: Tenant Lifecycle Provision Suspend Delete Export Semantic Contract
description: Source-backed draft semantic contract for the tenant lifecycle provision suspend delete export capability, recovered from historical source-lineage evidence as lineage. Facets cite verified source-import manifest rows; proof is hermetic/runtime lineage, not fresh USF proof.
aiGuidance: Source-backed draft. Facet descriptions are concise source-backed summaries citing verified manifest rows; proof is hermetic/stale, not current readiness. Enrich facet detail and publish fresh proof before generation; respect the hexagonal constraint in ADR 0005.

lifecycle.description: Lifecycle operations are explicit and ordered: provision creates the tenant, suspend coordinates data, storage, realm, then DSR after audit, and delete exports first and then coordinates storage, realm, DSR, and data. A coordinator sequences these subsystem transitions so destructive steps occur in a defined order.

stateModel.description: Tenant lifecycle state is represented by coordinated subsystem outcomes and export metadata: suspend returns coordinated [data, storage, realm, DSR]; delete returns coordinated [export, storage, realm, DSR, data] plus an export digest and key reference. The export is produced before deletion so a portable archive exists prior to destructive steps.

permissions.description: The capability is operator-controlled through platform tenant permissions with a system-admin actor context, and every lifecycle operation scopes by organisation identifier. Route handlers validate tenant identifiers before any lifecycle action.

contracts.description: The contract is a tenant-lifecycle coordinator exposing export, suspend-data/storage/realm/DSR, and delete-data/storage/realm/DSR operations plus the admin tenant route family for provision, suspend, delete, export, and import. The coordinator is the interface that orders subsystem calls.

validation.description: Delete refuses to continue after a subsystem coordination failure, so a failed realm deletion stops before DSR and data deletion. Tenant identifiers are validated at the route boundary and export must succeed before delete proceeds.

errorModel.description: Subsystem failures propagate as typed or ordinary errors and halt later destructive steps; for example a realm-deletion failure leaves the recorded calls at export, delete-storage, delete-realm with no further deletion. Failures fail closed so partial destruction does not silently complete.

auditModel.description: Suspend and delete emit audit before subsystem coordination, and both unit and runtime proof assert that the audit is invoked first. Lifecycle transitions are audited so provisioning, suspension, deletion, and export are attributable.

readinessModel.description: Runtime readiness is the tenant-lifecycle proof, which validates audit-first behaviour and the subsystem coordination order for suspend and delete. Readiness degrades explicitly when the lifecycle coordinator substrate is unavailable.

proof.description: Behaviour is exercised by tenant-lifecycle unit tests and a runtime proof asserting audit-first invocation and ordered subsystem coordination with export-before-delete. Proof is hermetic/runtime lineage; fresh USF proof pending USF-101/USF-59.

uiSemanticDefinition.description: No verified UI source path in the manifest yet; author the UI semantic model before generation.
